name: Sun Myung Moon
description: Sun Myung Moon (1920-2012) was a Korean religious leader, businessman, and anti-communist activist, best known as the founder of the Unification Church, officially named the Family Federation for World Peace and Unification. Moon claimed to be the Messiah, tasked with completing Jesus's unfinished mission of establishing God's Kingdom on Earth. He taught a unique interpretation of the Bible, emphasizing the Fall of Man through illicit love and the need for humanity to restore itself to God's original plan through true families centered on God. Key tenets include the Divine Principle, a new interpretation of scripture, and the concept of 'indemnity,' where individuals and humanity must make amends for past sins. Proponents point to the Church's global humanitarian efforts, interfaith initiatives, and emphasis on family values as evidence of its positive impact. Critics, however, raise concerns about Moon's self-proclaimed messianic status, the Church's controversial fundraising practices (including claims of brainwashing), the arranged 'mass weddings,' and its political activities, particularly its anti-communist stance and support for conservative causes. Mainstream religious scholars generally view the Unification Church as a new religious movement or cult, distinct from mainstream Christianity. Controversy surrounds the Church's vast business empire, which has been accused of exploiting members and generating profits used to support Moon's lavish lifestyle and political influence. The church's teachings diverge significantly from traditional Christian theology, particularly concerning the nature of God, Jesus, and salvation.
subtitle: Founder of the Unification Church

claims.claim: Sun Myung Moon was the Messiah.
claims.evidence: Moon's own pronouncements and interpretations of biblical prophecies, supported by followers' testimonies of his spiritual authority and divine mission.
claims.criticism: Mainstream Christian theology rejects the idea of a new Messiah after Jesus Christ. Critics point to inconsistencies in Moon's teachings and question the validity of his self-proclaimed messianic status.

claims.claim: The Divine Principle is the correct interpretation of the Bible.
claims.evidence: Followers believe the Divine Principle provides a deeper understanding of God's plan for humanity and resolves contradictions in traditional interpretations.
claims.criticism: Biblical scholars argue that the Divine Principle is a subjective and selective interpretation of scripture that distorts the original meaning and context of the Bible.

claims.claim: Mass weddings promote world peace and create 'true families'.
claims.evidence: The Church claims that these weddings transcend national, racial, and religious boundaries, fostering unity and harmony among diverse groups.
claims.criticism: Critics argue that these weddings are coercive and exploitative, forcing individuals into arranged marriages with strangers based on Moon's authority, and raising concerns about individual autonomy and consent.

keyFigures.name: Sun Myung Moon
keyFigures.role: Founder and leader of the Unification Church

keyFigures.name: Steven Hassan
keyFigures.role: Cult expert and critic of the Unification Church

timeline.event: Sun Myung Moon is born in present-day North Korea.
Moon founds the Unification Church in Seoul, South Korea.
The Unification Church expands globally and becomes increasingly controversial.
Sun Myung Moon dies at age 92.

sources.author: George D. Chryssides
sources.work: The Unification Church: Its Theology and Spiritual Appeal
sources.title: George D. Chryssides

sources.title: Nansook Hong
